metadata.title: Advanced Therapy Medicinal Products and the Committee for Advanced Therapies (CAT)
metadata.notes: Short 6-page informational/promotional leaflet. Limited regulatory rules — primarily defines framework, incentives, and institutional roles. Not a technical guideline.

rules.rule_text: Advanced therapy medicinal products (ATMPs) in the EU must be developed within the legal and regulatory framework established by Regulation (EC) No 1394/2007.
rules.verbatim_quote: Regulation (EC) No 1394/2007 established the legal and regulatory framework for advanced therapy medicinal products (ATMPs) in the European Union.

rules.rule_text: ATMPs are classified exclusively as gene-therapy, cell-therapy, or tissue-engineered medicinal products under EU law.
rules.verbatim_quote: ATMPs are defined as gene- and cell-therapy and tissue-engineered medicinal products.

rules.rule_text: Marketing authorisation applications for ATMPs must be evaluated by the Committee for Advanced Therapies (CAT) at EMA, which is the main scientific committee responsible for this evaluation.
rules.verbatim_quote: The CAT, established by the ATMP Regulation, is the European Medicines Agency's main scientific committee for the evaluation of applications for marketing authorisation for ATMPs.

rules.rule_text: Quality and non-clinical data for ATMP certification procedures must undergo scientific evaluation by the CAT.
rules.verbatim_quote: Other activities of the CAT include the scientific evaluation of quality and non-clinical data for certification procedures, contributing to scientific advice for ATMPs, and providing scientific recommendations on ATMP classification.

rules.rule_text: Scientific guidelines governing gene therapy, cell therapy, and tissue-engineered products are prepared by the CAT; developers must follow CAT-issued guidelines.
rules.verbatim_quote: The CAT is also responsible for the preparation of scientific guidelines in the fields of gene- and cell-therapy and tissue-engineered products.

rules.rule_text: ATMP developers are strongly encouraged to engage in early and repetitive interactions with national authorities and EMA/CAT to determine the appropriate regulatory and scientific route, including ATMP classification, certification, scientific advice, and PRIME.
rules.source_section: ATMPs in the EU – overcoming the challenges to bring ATMPs to the patient
rules.verbatim_quote: Early and repetitive interactions between the ATMP developers and the national authorities and the EMA / CAT are strongly encouraged. EMA / CAT will advise companies on the most appropriate regulatory and scientific route to be followed. This includes interactions with the EMA's Innovation Task Force and the SME office, orphan designation, ATMP classification and certification, scientific advice and PRIME.

rules.rule_text: Developers of ATMPs may obtain scientific recommendations on ATMP classification, fee reductions for scientific advice, and evaluation/certification of quality and non-clinical data as regulatory incentives under Regulation (EC) No 1394/2007.
rules.verbatim_quote: The regulation provides incentives to companies involved in developing ATMPs, including: fee reductions for scientific advice; scientific recommendations on ATMP classification; evaluation and certification of quality and non-clinical data.
